warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.sourceTitle: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015
rules.notes: GB 2760-2015 is the national standard for the use of food additives in China. Specific limits for various additives and food categories are detailed within this standard.
rules.evidenceQuote: Standard for Food Additive Use - GB2760-2015

rules.sourceTitle: National Health Commission of the People's Republic of China
rules.notes: The National Health Commission is responsible for developing and reviewing national food safety standards. Specific limits and requirements are detailed in these standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: 食品安全标准与监测评估司; 食品安全国家标准审评委员会秘书处

rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA) Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods. Users are encouraged to consult the database and its Preamble for specific provisions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.parameter: Contaminants (e.g., mycotoxins, metals like arsenic, lead, mercury)
rules.limit: Verification required via Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995)
rules.notes: Codex establishes Maximum Levels (MLs) for contaminants in food and feed commodities to protect human health. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.notes: The database provides information on approved food additives and their conditions of use, based on Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii), their toxins and metabolites (e.g., staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, and process hygiene criteria for foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية - بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة
rules.notes: This regulation covers general labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs, which would include information such as ingredients, nutritional facts, and potentially warnings.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.sourceTitle: GSO 9:2022 Labeling of prepackaged food stuffs
rules.notes: This technical regulation sets out the general requirements for labeling of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified by consulting the full document.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: Mandatory expiration dates for food products

rules.foodCategory: Foodstuffs, Food ingredients (includes Processed foods, Seasoning, Snack food)
rules.notes: This technical regulation establishes microbiological limits for various foodstuffs and food ingredients intended for human consumption and use in the food industry. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the full document.
rules.evidenceQuote: This GSO technical regulation is concerned with microbiological limits for some foodstuffs intended for human consumption and for some food ingredients used in food industry.

rules.notes: Specific permitted food additives and their maximum limits for various food categories must be verified against the detailed schedules and provisions of the FSSAI Food Additives Regulations.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Food_Additives_Regulations

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: Mandatory declaration of specified allergens on food product labels is required as per the FSSAI Labelling and Display Regulations. The exact list of allergens and declaration format must be verified against the regulation.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: Compendium of Labelling and Display Regulations
rules.notes: All aspects of labelling and display for food products must comply with the FSSAI Labelling and Display Regulations. Specific requirements for font size, information placement, and other declarations need to be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Compendium_Labelling_Display

rules.sourceTitle: SE Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 Surat Edaran Nomor 2 Tahun 2026 tentang Ketentuan Produksi, Importasi, Registrasi, dan Peredaran Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)
rules.notes: This circular specifically addresses Dinitrogen Monoxide (N2O) as a food additive, indicating that BPOM regulates the production, import, registration, and circulation of food additives. Specific provisions must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Bahan Tambahan Pangan Dinitrogen Monoksida (N2O)

rules.parameter: Permitted Food Additives and their limits
rules.notes: BPOM regulates food additives in processed foods. Specific permitted additives and their maximum limits must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.

rules.notes: BPOM requires allergen declarations for processed foods. Specific requirements for allergen lists and labelling must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.notes: BPOM regulates contaminant limits for processed foods. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.sourceTitle: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan
rules.notes: This regulation defines the categories for processed foods, which is fundamental for applying specific additive, contaminant, or other limits and for correct labelling.
rules.evidenceQuote: PERKABPOM Nomor 34 Tahun 2019 Tentang Kategori Pangan

rules.notes: BPOM sets general labelling requirements and warnings for processed foods. Specific requirements must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.notes: BPOM regulates microbiological limits for processed foods. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.notes: BPOM may regulate salt/sodium limits for processed foods. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the relevant 'Pangan Olahan' regulations available on the JDIH BPOM website.
rules.evidenceQuote: Pangan Olahan 281 Peraturan

rules.parameter: Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.limit: Not to be directly added when manufacturing the kueh; can be directly added to fillings and toppings within prescribed regulatory limits
rules.notes: For details on the use of preservatives in kueh, download the provided slides.
rules.evidenceQuote: Class II chemical preservatives, such as sorbic acid and benzoic acid, should not be directly added when manufacturing the kueh. Class II chemical preservatives can only be directly added, within the prescribed regulatory limits, to fillings and toppings in kuehs.

rules.limit: Must be assessed for safety by SFA before use
rules.notes: Only SFA-assessed additives are permitted.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Only permitted additives are allowed; verification required
rules.notes: Verification required using SFA's List of Permitted Food Additives or Food Additives Search tool. Non-permitted additives must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: Only food additives that have been assessed by SFA are permitted for use in food products sold in Singapore.

rules.limit: Different limits apply depending on additive and food type; verification required
rules.notes: Use the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives to learn more about the different types of additives and the corresponding regulations that impose the limits.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.limit: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits
rules.evidenceQuote: All food businesses offering food for sale in Singapore must comply with the regulatory limits on food additives.

rules.limit: Use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to determine permitted levels
rules.notes: Applies if two or more Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid or benzoic acid) are used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food (e.g. sorbic acid or benzoic acid), use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels that should be used.

rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: All food businesses must comply. Products exceeding limits must not be imported or sold.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.limit: Verification required from SFA's linked documents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refers to various limits for different types of contaminants. Businesses should be aware of common contaminants and conduct laboratory tests as needed.
rules.evidenceQuote: There are various limits imposed on different types of contaminants or incidental constituents in food. For more details, please refer to the relevant documents below:

rules.limit: Verification required from SFA's linked document
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to the 'Heavy metals' document for specific limits.

rules.limit: Verification required from SFA's linked document

rules.notes: Refer to the 'Other incidental constituents' document for specific limits.

rules.limit: Verification required from Singapore's regulatory limits and Codex MRLs

rules.limit: Verification required from SFA's linked document and website for MRLs

rules.notes: SFA has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses.
rules.evidenceQuote: SFA has also assessed the risk of the presence of other veterinary drug residues in various food commodities, and has established a list of maximum residue limits (MRLs) for veterinary drug residues for compliance by food businesses with effect from 1 May 2020.

rules.limit: Must be declared in the list of ingredients on food labels
rules.evidenceQuote: All ingredients and additives used in food products must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels.

rules.limit: Verification required from SFA's linked documents
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Refer to 'Microbiological standards' documents for 'Ready-to-eat food' and 'Non-ready-to-eat food'.

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged foods, Processed Foods, Seasoning, Snack Food

rules.limit: Recommended labeling if present as ingredient or food additive

rules.notes: These allergens are recommended for labeling when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: food allergen labeling is also recommended for the products that contain any of the following allergens as an ingredient or food additive: (1) Cephalopods and cephalopod products... (2) Spiral shells and Spiral shell products thereof... (3) Seeds and seed products... (4) Kiwis and kiwi products thereof...

rules.foodCategory: Prepackaged foods, Processed Foods, Seasoning, Snack Food
rules.parameter: Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish

rules.notes: These allergens require mandatory labeling when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.
rules.evidenceQuote: For prepackaged foods, food allergen labeling is required for the products that contain any of the following 11 allergens as an ingredient or food additive... Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA regulations and inventories
rules.notes: Includes substances listed in 21 CFR Parts 172, 173, 73, 74, 182, 184, 181, and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA and JECFA.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Substances Added to Food inventory includes the following types of ingredients regulated by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA): Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*. Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.parameter: Prohibited substances and delisted color additives
rules.notes: Prohibited substances are listed in 21 CFR Part 189. Delisted color additives are in 21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30.
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS"). Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame
rules.notes: Sesame was added as the 9th major food allergen by the FASTER Act, effective January 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.

rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400

rules.limit: Verification required against FDA's Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and associated guidance/regulations
rules.notes: Includes tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels.

rules.limit: Must meet FDA's defined standard for gluten content
rules.notes: FDA issued a final rule defining "gluten-free" for food labeling on August 2, 2013, and compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods on August 12, 2020.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.notes: Can be used to address unavoidable cross-contact if good manufacturing practices are followed and must be truthful and not misleading. Not a substitute for GMPs.
rules.evidenceQuote: Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact...

rules.limit: Facilities must implement written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate labeling
rules.notes: Part of the "Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food" rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117).
rules.evidenceQuote: The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. For example, the FDA requires facilities to put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact between products that contain allergens and those that are not supposed to contain them and to ensure that the products are accurately labeled with respect to allergens.

rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: Declaration methods: in parentheses following ingredient name (e.g., "lecithin (soy)") OR in a "contains" statement immediately after/next to ingredient list (e.g., "Contains wheat, milk, and soy."). Specific type/species required for tree nuts, fish, and Crustacean shellfish.
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient... — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement.

rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.notes: Must be declared on food labels when present at or above this concentration.
rules.evidenceQuote: they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.
